free_text: Fast-Neutron-Spectrum Measurements for the Thick-
Target 9Be(d,n)10B Reaction at Ed = 7 MeV
Full report and tables
Summary, graphs given
The Argonne National Laboratory Fast-
Neutron Generator facility was employed to produce
pulsed and bunched deuteron beams
The beryllium target wafers were 0.05-cm thick disks
(more than thick enough to stop 7-MeV deuterons) in
target chamber of low mass design. The side walls of
assembly are 0.05 cm thick stainless steel. The end
caps where the beryllium target wafers are situated
were also 0.05 cm thick and they were made either of
stainless steel or tantalum. Most measurements were
performed with the beryllium target wafer placed
perpendicular to the incident deuteron beam.
In measurements near 90deg of a special target cap
which placed a beryllium wafer at 45deg to the deuteron
beam was used.
Neutron detector was a 2.54 cm diameter by
0.635 cm thick NE-102A plastic scintillator. No pulse
shape discrimination was possible. Two other
scintillation detectors with NE-213 liquid scintillator
with thickness of 2 cm and diameter of 12.7 cm were
used as monitor detectors
The distance from the beryllium target to the
center of the scintillator was measured to be 503.2+-
0.2 cm. Deuteron beam was pulsed and bunched with
nominally 2 ns time resolutions for gamma rays.
Statistical uncertainty
Compiled by S.H.
OS. Minor BIB changes
